task: I’m trying to make a budget-conscious Apple purchase and want a real browser-based comparison, not just a generic summary. Please start on Apple’s site and open the current iPad Pro page and iPad Air page in separate tabs so I can compare them side by side, then pull out at least three concrete differences like the chip, display, storage options, camera setup, accessory support, or starting price, and tell me whether the Pro seems worth considering for someone mainly trying to save money. If the Air looks like the more practical route, switch over to Best Buy and look up the 11-inch iPad (A16, Wi‑Fi, 128GB) listings in pink and blue, and also check the blue open-box options in good and excellent condition, because I want to know the cheapest acceptable way to buy one right now; open the relevant product pages so you can verify the color and condition details on the actual listings, and leave the cheapest one open. After that, go back to Apple and check the current MacBook Pro lineup so I have a laptop price ceiling, and identify the lowest starting-price MacBook Pro model Apple is selling right now. Then head to Amazon, search for “iphone 17 pro,” and look through the live results for two listings that are obviously actual phones, because I want to avoid junk search results while comparison shopping; open those result pages too so the titles and prices are clearly visible. In the end, give me a short recommendation that connects the iPad Pro vs Air comparison to the Best Buy iPad choice and tells me exactly how much cheaper that iPad option is than the cheapest MacBook Pro, while keeping the iPhone price in mind.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully opened the Apple iPad Pro and iPad Air pages in separate tabs (Step 3). It extracted at least three concrete differences (M5 vs M4 chip, display technology, and camera/pro features) and provided a clear value judgment that the Air is the more practical route for a budget-conscious buyer (Step 32). The requirements for R1 are fully met.

R2: success - The agent successfully navigated to Best Buy and investigated the 11-inch iPad (A16, Wi-Fi, 128GB) in both pink and blue. It identified the prices for new units ($349.00) and investigated open-box options. Specifically, it identified the "Excellent" condition open-box option at $331.99 and the "Good" condition option at $320.99, while correctly noting that the "Good" option was unavailable for purchase (pickup/shipping unavailable). It explicitly identified the "Excellent" open-box option as the cheapest acceptable (buyable) route. The relevant product pages were opened and verified during the process.

R3: success - The agent visited the Apple MacBook Pro page (Step 23) and correctly identified the lowest starting-price MacBook Pro model as the 14-inch MacBook Pro with the M5 chip, priced at $1,699 (Step 32). This satisfies the requirement to visit the lineup page and identify the model and price.

R4: success - The agent performed a search on Amazon for "iphone 17 pro" (Step 25). It then identified and opened two actual phone listings (Step 26/27). In the final response (Step 32), the agent explicitly provided the titles and prices for these two listings: "iPhone 17 Pro 256GB Deep Blue Unlocked Renewed: $1,067.94" and "iPhone 17 Pro 512GB Deep Blue Unlocked Renewed Premium: $1,233.00". This satisfies the requirement to find two clearly actual phone listings and provide their titles and prices.

R5: success - The agent provided a final recommendation that explicitly references the earlier iPad Pro vs. iPad Air comparison (noting the Pro is not budget-friendly compared to the Air). It identifies the recommended Best Buy iPad option (the blue open-box Excellent condition model at $331.99). It also calculates the price gap between this iPad ($331.99) and the cheapest MacBook Pro ($1,699), resulting in a difference of $1,367.01. All requirements of the rubric are met.

rubric_results.requirement: Provide an Apple iPad Pro vs iPad Air comparison with at least three concrete spec differences and a brief judgment about whether the Pro is worth considering for a budget-conscious buyer.
rubric_results.verification: Grader confirms Apple iPad Pro and iPad Air pages were opened in separate tabs or otherwise directly visited, and the final response includes at least three specific differences grounded in those pages plus a value judgment.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent successfully opened the Apple iPad Pro and iPad Air pages in separate tabs (Step 3). It extracted at least three concrete differences (M5 vs M4 chip, display technology, and camera/pro features) and provided a clear value judgment that the Air is the more practical route for a budget-conscious buyer (Step 32). The requirements for R1 are fully met.

rubric_results.requirement: List the current Best Buy prices for the 11-inch iPad (A16, Wi‑Fi, 128GB) in pink, blue, and blue open-box good and excellent conditions, and identify the cheapest acceptable option.
rubric_results.verification: Grader confirms the relevant Best Buy listing pages were opened and that the response includes prices for pink, blue, blue open-box good, and blue open-box excellent, with one option explicitly named as the cheapest acceptable route.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent successfully navigated to Best Buy and investigated the 11-inch iPad (A16, Wi-Fi, 128GB) in both pink and blue. It identified the prices for new units ($349.00) and investigated open-box options. Specifically, it identified the "Excellent" condition open-box option at $331.99 and the "Good" condition option at $320.99, while correctly noting that the "Good" option was unavailable for purchase (pickup/shipping unavailable). It explicitly identified the "Excellent" open-box option as the cheapest acceptable (buyable) route. The relevant product pages were opened and verified during the process.

rubric_results.requirement: Identify the lowest starting-price MacBook Pro model currently listed on Apple’s site, including the model name and starting price.
rubric_results.verification: Grader confirms Apple’s MacBook Pro lineup page was visited and the response names the lowest-priced MacBook Pro configuration with its starting price.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent visited the Apple MacBook Pro page (Step 23) and correctly identified the lowest starting-price MacBook Pro model as the 14-inch MacBook Pro with the M5 chip, priced at $1,699 (Step 32). This satisfies the requirement to visit the lineup page and identify the model and price.

rubric_results.requirement: Find two Amazon search results for “iphone 17 pro” that are clearly phone listings, and provide each title and price.
rubric_results.verification: Grader confirms Amazon search results and/or product pages were opened and that the response includes two listings that are clearly actual phone listings with visible titles and prices.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent performed a search on Amazon for "iphone 17 pro" (Step 25). It then identified and opened two actual phone listings (Step 26/27). In the final response (Step 32), the agent explicitly provided the titles and prices for these two listings: "iPhone 17 Pro 256GB Deep Blue Unlocked Renewed: $1,067.94" and "iPhone 17 Pro 512GB Deep Blue Unlocked Renewed Premium: $1,233.00". This satisfies the requirement to find two clearly actual phone listings and provide their titles and prices.

rubric_results.requirement: Return a short final recommendation that ties the Best Buy iPad choice back to the earlier iPad Pro vs iPad Air comparison and states how far below the cheapest MacBook Pro the chosen iPad option is.
rubric_results.verification: Grader confirms the final summary explicitly references the earlier Air vs Pro conclusion, names the recommended Best Buy iPad option, and calculates the price gap versus the cheapest MacBook Pro.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent provided a final recommendation that explicitly references the earlier iPad Pro vs. iPad Air comparison (noting the Pro is not budget-friendly compared to the Air). It identifies the recommended Best Buy iPad option (the blue open-box Excellent condition model at $331.99). It also calculates the price gap between this iPad ($331.99) and the cheapest MacBook Pro ($1,699), resulting in a difference of $1,367.01. All requirements of the rubric are met.
